2606.11166.title: Flaws in the LLM Automation Narrative
2606.11166.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly described as performing at the level of human experts on knowledge economy tasks. These claims are primarily based on how LLMs perform on benchmarking tasks that measure average performance across standardized datasets. Primary limitations of many benchmarking tasks are that they often measure performance based on content directly included in LLM training data, and they frequently do not assess the reliability of LLM performance or the magnitude of LLM errors. However, in high stakes contexts, these qualities are critically important. Through a novel LLM benchmarking task that requires writing computer code to complete a data analysis task, we compare the performance of a frontier LLM against submissions from human experts and explicitly measure the variance of responses and the magnitude of errors. Our study reveals that the human experts perform better on average on a range of metrics and demonstrate less variability in performance. Our results provide evidence that LLMs do not consistently perform at the level of human experts and demonstrate the importance of measuring variance and assessing error magnitude in LLM benchmark evaluations.
2606.11166.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被描述為在知識經濟任務中達到人類專家的水平。這些說法主要基於LLMs在基準任務中的表現，這些任務測量的是在標準化數據集上的平均表現。許多基準任務的主要限制在於，它們通常根據直接包含在LLM訓練數據中的內容來衡量表現，並且經常不評估LLM表現的可靠性或LLM錯誤的大小。然而，在高風險的情境中，這些特質是至關重要的。通過一項新穎的LLM基準任務，要求編寫計算機代碼以完成數據分析任務，我們將前沿LLM的表現與人類專家的提交進行比較，並明確測量反應的變異性和錯誤的大小。我們的研究顯示，人類專家在多個指標上的平均表現更佳，並且表現的變異性較小。我們的結果提供了證據表明LLMs並不總是能夠達到人類專家的水平，並且顯示了在LLM基準評估中測量變異性和評估錯誤大小的重要性。
2606.11166.author: George Perrett et.al.
2606.11166.authors: George Perrett, Javae Elliott, Jennifer Hill, Marc Scott
